channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from global sources

item.title: 🇮🇷🇷🇺 Iran FM Araghchi heads to Moscow as US-Iran peace talks stall
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to meet President Vladimir Putin in St. Petersburg on April 27 after diplomatic efforts in Pakistan and Oman failed to advance peace negotiations with the US.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Nearly 214,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine identified by media investigation
item.description: Mediazona and BBC Russian service have confirmed the identities of 213,858 Russian military personnel killed in Ukraine, with over 5,000 names added since early April. Actual figures are likely significantly higher.

item.title: 🇲🇱⚔️ Mali defense minister killed in insurgent attack as Russia withdraws from Kidal
item.description: Mali Defense Minister Sadio Camara, who had close ties to Russia and Wagner, was killed in an attack on his home near Bamako. Russia's Africa Corps simultaneously withdrew from the northern city of Kidal after clashes with separatist rebels.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇰🇵 Russian defense minister visits Pyongyang, opens memorial for North Korean soldiers
item.description: Defense Minister Andrei Belousov awarded medals to North Korean soldiers who fought in Kursk Oblast and opened a memorial in Pyongyang, as Moscow and Pyongyang continue expanding military cooperation under their strategic treaty.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇷🚗 Chinese EVs surge in South Korea with 33% market share as fuel prices climb
item.description: China-made electric vehicles now account for one in three new registrations in South Korea, up from 4.7% in 2022, driven by Tesla Shanghai models and competitive pricing amid elevated fuel costs linked to the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇰🇵🇷🇺 North Korea Opens Memorial Museum for Troops Killed Fighting for Russia
item.description: Kim Jong Un attended the opening ceremony in Pyongyang alongside Russian Defense Minister Beloushov and Duma Speaker Volodin, marking one year since North Korean troops helped repel Ukraines Kursk incursion.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Cancels US Peace Delegation Trip to Pakistan After Iranian FM Departs
item.description: President Trump canceled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushners planned trip to Islamabad for Iran peace talks, suggesting negotiations continue by phone instead.

item.title: 🛢️💰 Oil Prices Surge 44% Since Iran War Began as Hormuz Transit Remains Blocked
item.description: West Texas Intermediate reached $96.50/barrel and Brent crude $107.75, up 44-48% since the Iran war began, as ceasefire talks stall and tankers remain stranded in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles State Visit to US to Proceed Despite Washington Shooting
item.description: Buckingham Palace confirmed King Charles and Queen Camilla's four-day state visit to the United States will proceed as planned starting Monday, following discussions with US officials after a shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner attended by President Trump.

item.title: 🌏 Asia May Emerge as Unexpected Beneficiary from Iran War Fallout
item.description: While Asia has been highly vulnerable to the Iran war due to its dependence on Middle East energy, analysts suggest the region could emerge as a long-term winner from trends accelerated by the conflict, including supply chain diversification and energy transition.

item.title: 🇰🇵🇷🇺⚔️ North Korea Opens Memorial Honoring Troops Killed Fighting for Russia
item.description: Pyongyang unveiled a memorial for North Korean soldiers killed fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, with Kim Jong-un emphasizing the need to strengthen ties with Moscow into a "powerful bulwark." This marks the first official acknowledgment and commemoration of North Korean combat deaths in the Ukraine war.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱⚠️ Hezbollah and Israel Trade Accusations Over Lebanon Ceasefire Violations
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah exchanged blame over violations of the fragile ceasefire in Lebanon, with each side accusing the other of provocations as the truce remains on shaky ground amid the broader regional conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸💼 China Uses Trade Truce With US to Expand Economic Leverage Before Summit
item.description: Beijing has used the trade truce with Washington to broaden its legal leverage, supply chain controls, and critical technology restrictions, expanding its toolkit of retaliatory economic measures ahead of a potential summit between the nations next month.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Security Under Review After Gunman Breaches White House Correspondents Dinner
item.description: Law enforcement officials are reassessing security protocols after an armed suspect was able to get close to an event attended by President Trump and senior officials, raising questions about protection arrangements.

item.title: 💰⚔️ Global Military Spending Hits Record $2.9 Trillion Despite US Cuts
item.description: World military expenditure rose 2.9% in 2025 to a record high, with the US accounting for less as Trump halted Ukraine aid while other nations ramped up defense budgets amid rising global tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US-Iran Conflict Stuck in No War No Peace Limbo as Both Sides Wait
item.description: With peace talks stalled and neither side willing to escalate further, the US-Iran standoff has settled into an uneasy stalemate where each is betting it can outlast the other.

item.title: ⛪🌍 Pope Leo and the Shift From Just War to Peace Activism
item.description: The popes criticism of the Iran war reflects a broader transformation in Catholic teaching on warfare, driven by the devastating power of modern weaponry and the collapse of traditional just war theory.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Cancels Pakistan Peace Mission, Says Iran Can Call Instead
item.description: President Trump called off a planned U.S. envoy trip to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, saying Tehran can telephone if it wants to negotiate. Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi headed to Moscow as diplomatic efforts stall.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇵🇰 Iranian Foreign Minister Returns to Pakistan, Seeks to Revive Ceasefire Talks
item.description: Iran's Abbas Araghchi made a brief return to Islamabad as Pakistani leaders scrambled to reignite negotiations. The diplomatic push comes amid continued hostilities and a stalled Strait of Hormuz blockade.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇪🇸 NATO Says No Provision to Expel Members After Pentagon Email on Spain
item.description: NATO responded to reports of a Pentagon email suggesting options to suspend Spain from the alliance over perceived lack of support for Iran operations, stating there is no mechanism to expel members.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱 Israel Issues Evacuation Warning for Seven Lebanese Towns Beyond Buffer Zone
item.description: The Israeli military warned residents to leave seven towns north of the Litani River after a soldier was killed. Strikes killed 14 people in Lebanon as the ceasefire remains fragile.

item.title: 🛢️💰 Oil Prices Climb Nearly 2% as US-Iran Peace Talks Stall
item.description: Crude oil extended gains as diplomatic efforts between Washington and Tehran faltered while shipments through the Strait of Hormuz remained limited, tightening global supplies.

item.title: 🇭🇰💼 Hong Kong Plans T+1 Settlement Shift to Sharpen Financial Hub Edge
item.description: After more than 30 years of T+2 settlement, Hong Kong plans to move to T+1 settlement. The shift aims to strengthen Hong Kong competitive position as a global financial hub amid intensifying regional competition.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇮🇷🛢️ US Sanctions Major Chinese Refinery Over Iranian Oil Purchases
item.description: The Trump administration sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical's Dalian facility, one of China's largest independent refineries, along with 40 shipping companies for transporting Iranian oil. The move is part of a "maximum pressure" campaign to cut off Iran's key revenue source ahead of Trump's planned meeting with Xi Jinping.

item.title: 🇪🇸🇺🇸🇪🇺 Pentagon Email Floats Suspending Spain From NATO Over Iran Stance
item.description: An internal Pentagon email outlines options to punish NATO allies who failed to support US operations against Iran, including suspending Spain and reviewing the US position on Britain's Falkland Islands claim. NATO officials say Trump cannot unilaterally expel members.
